mod 6 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey, guys, welcome back. This is going to be a walkthrough of the Module 6 workbook.
We just saw the main lesson from the sales sequence. Just as a reminder, this is the
sequence that occurs from the moment we open up cart until 48 hours before the close.
This is the part of your launch that will usually represent the greatest lull, but if
engineered correctly like we're going to do with the themeplates below, they could still
have the power to really bring in those steady conversions, so that sales don't take that
dramatic nosedive between your initial launch and your close as well as set up the
conversion for the closing sequence just by reinforcing and recontextualizing the beliefs
you instilled during the prelaunch. Now what I mean by recontextualizing is all those
beliefs that you delivered in the prelaunch are now contextualized with your product
now present. That's the drop of the sales sequence too. Integrate, throw your product
into that mixing bowl, and make sure that it blends in well with all the beliefs you've
already created for them. That's a good little metaphor and creates a tasty little treat for
your prospect.
What we're not doing here is bringing new sales arguments into the mix. This is only
going to dilute all that important work you've already done and overwhelm your
prospect into a state of analysis paralysis. You'll also want to avoid bringing time
emergency into place early and definitely not before 48 hours. That's why we have the
distinction between the sales sequence and the closing sequence, because if you start
talking about just three days left, 72 hours left, whatever it is, that actually has that
countereffect and makes your prospect feel like they have time to put off the decision.
They're like, "Great, I'll think about this in three days." Not only are they procrastinating,
but you're losing that momentum that you've generated. That's really the most crucial
part here, is keeping that foot on the accelerator and not letting them off the hook.
With all that said, here's what we are coaching. Once again, we're coaching
identification with being a buyer, letting your prospect see themselves as a buyer. We're
selling recommitment, and we're closing that consistency loop we set up earlier during
the confirmation stage. We're revealing risk, so we're not reversing it yet. We're doing
the risk revealer, which gets our prospect extremely intimate with the stakes and the
costs of going backwards. That's what keeps the momentum going. Then we are
reinforcing the beliefs, reframes, outcomes, those slaughter cows and those ahas using
proof. Our offer is now here, people have used it. We have some testimonials, we have
some credibility. That's a part of how we recontextualize.
With all that said, we are going to dive into some questions from our 47 Questions
Workbook. As always, if you've done them before, this is your chance to review them
and really add some more insights that you've generated since that initial time you
spent with the workbook, but here we go. Parallel realities, divergent roads. This is a big
thing that's really going to help you reveal that risk, the cost of going backwards if they
don't buy your product as well as help create safety in the outcome. Safety in the
outcome comes from predictability, from seeing what it will look like, and feeling at east
with that future. The point is to make this look extremely realistic. Don't use too much
hyperbole when detailing these parallel realities. Be very real, be very grounded. If parts
of the process are going to suck and not be seamless, say they're going to suck and not
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

mod 6 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 3
be seamless because when you're honest about that, that gives credibility that when
you are talking about the positive outcomes and the positive results, that that's true.
You want to be specific here. You also want to be real and grounded. By all means, talk
about the positive outcomes that they're going to experience in those time frames, but
ground it to something that's extremely realistic for them and also acknowledge parts of
it that may not be so much fun. In vivid detail, what does success look like three days
from now, three weeks, three months, 12 months? Then we're going to reverse it. If
they don't buy your product, what's it going to look like in three days, three weeks,
three months, and 12 months?
A few other additional questions. What beliefs, micro commitments have they agreed to
that you can call them out and stay consistent with? What are the questions a perfect
buyer would ask, or have asked before saying, "Yes?" This goes beyond that general
Q&A. What are those questions that people are asking before they buy? You don't want
to have a general FAQ where people have asked us, "What have buyers asked?" because
that's what's really going to help current prospects identify with buyers. Yeah, I have
that question on my mind. That person had it too, and they became a buyer. It creates
that alignment.
What case studies, stories, proof elements can you link the beliefs and reframes to? This
is where we recontextualize it, place those beliefs in context with what you're offer will
deliver or has been proven to deliver based on case studies or testimonials. What mid-
cart bonus can you offer for 24 hours that would either set them up for that next thing
after completing your program? What this does is it presupposes A, that they've bought
and B, that they've achieved the success and the outcomes that your course is
promising. Now they're ready for this next thing, this graduation gift, if you will. You can
also have a mid-cart bonus that helps them achieve an exciting outcome that's
presented in your case study or at least complements your core program in helping get
that specific outcome that you outlined.
What's the real hyperspecific cost of going backward? That's the risk revealer. If I don't
learn how to play electric guitar or acoustic guitar, sorry, what's that cost of going
backward? I'm still going to go on my annual camping trip. There's still going to be a
bunch of buddies around the fire, and I'm still going to be wishing that I was the one
getting all that attention, playing Green Day's Time of Your Life while everyone else
soaks it in and looks upon this guitar playing god with envy. That's, to me, that moment
of highest tension. The cost of going backwards will usually relate with that. It will
usually mean continuing to experience that moment of highest tension we spoke about
earlier.
What's that big benefit of moving forward? This is the future pace. We did the exercise
just a little earlier where we gave different times frames for that. That's a big core piece
of the sales sequence. What are those three biggest initial objections that eventual
buyers have, and how can you frame that skepticism as a positive trait, the trait of
someone who eventually invests? I want you to invite skepticism from your prospects
and not frame skepticism as a characteristic or an action of someone who is not a buyer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

mod 6 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)

Page 3 of 3
I want you to instead see how you can include skepticism, and concern, and questions,
and all these things of someone who is a buyer, someone who really, really wants to get
that result, but they've been maybe burned before. They don't have that level of
believability anymore. Within every skeptic there is someone lurking within that want to
overcome it. No one wants to remain skeptical perpetually. Skepticism isn't a bad thing,
and how can you include that as an aspect of a buyer as a positive trait?
We are going to show you how to work these elements in in a second. We're going to
look at a themeplate called The Upgraded FAQ, which is really going to help you ground
some of these elements that we're coaching, so take a breather. Fill out these questions
because they are going to serve you and make the next steps in this lesson a lot more
easier if you already have that in front of you. Take some time to do that right now and
meet me in Themeplate 11 when you're ready. I'll see you in a bit.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
